design_philosophy.core_loop: Assign limited-use skills to individual lemmings in real-time to guide them safely through obstacles to the exit
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited skill uses) vs. puzzle complexity; time pressure from level timer
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Orchestrating small creatures through dangerous environments by strategically empowering them with temporary abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Requires pausing for inspection but skill assignment only works in real-time, forcing players to make reactive decisions under pressure. Emphasizes puzzle elegance over action reflex—most failure comes from poor planning, not execution speed

difficulty.continues_system: Levels can be replayed individually; failure triggers level restart, not game over
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpoint system; player progresses through four difficulty categories sequentially
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can adjust lemming release rate in real-time to control pacing; must solve puzzle optimally within predetermined skill constraints

progression.upgrade_system: Eight distinct skills unlock and become available at varying quantities per level: Climber, Floater, Bomber, Blocker, Builder, Basher, Miner, Digger
progression.unlock_mechanism: Skills are introduced gradually across levels; progression through four difficulty tiers (ordered by complexity)
replayability_hooks: Multiple skill assignment solutions per level encourage experimentation
Percentage-based save targets (not all lemmings required) allow partial success progression
Subsequent playthroughs optimize for higher rescue percentages
